title: T-107 — Line List screen (SET-018 §12.2)
prompt:
# T-107 — Line List screen (SET-018 §12.2)

PRD: docs/prd/02-SETTINGS-PRD.md §12.2 (Infrastructure CRUD UI table)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Build the Line List screen from §12.2 — list `production_lines` with a per-row machine sequence preview (ordered chip row) and filter by warehouse + status. Activation guarded by V-SET-62 (≥1 machine).

## Implementation contract
1. Page at apps/web/app/(admin)/settings/infra/lines/page.tsx (Server Component) fetches production_lines + their assigned machines (joined order_seq).
2. Each row shows: line name, default_location_id breadcrumb, machine sequence chips (ordered) up to 6, overflow `+N more`, status badge.
3. Toolbar: warehouse filter, status filter, bulk activate/deactivate buttons calling T-029 actions.
4. Bulk activate flow surfaces V-SET-62 NO_MACHINE error per row (shown inline); successful rows proceed.
5. RBAC: requires `settings.infra.read` for view, `settings.infra.update` for bulk actions.

## Files
- apps/web/app/(admin)/settings/infra/lines/page.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(admin)/settings/infra/lines/page.test.tsx [create]

## Acceptance criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` array.

## Test strategy
1. RED: RTL test mounting page with mocked lines (one with 0 machines, one with 4 machines); assert chips render, V-SET-62 error path on activate.
   `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run apps/web/app/(admin)/settings/infra/lines/page.test.tsx`
2. RED must fail.
3. GREEN: implement Server Component + chips + bulk action wiring.
4. Re-run RED + `pnpm typecheck` + `pnpm lint`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not import @radix-ui/* outside packages/ui (§0).
- Do not bypass V-SET-62 client-side; rely on T-029 server enforcement.
- Do not implement Line Edit drag-drop (separate SET-019 screen).

Stack: TS + Next.js App Router + Drizzle + pnpm + vitest + Playwright + Server Actions. PRD: docs/prd/02-SETTINGS-PRD.md v3.5.

## Prototype reference
**Source:** `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/org-screens.jsx:192-252`
**Label:** `warehouses_screen`
**Component type:** page-layout

> **No exact prototype** — closest visual pattern: `warehouses_screen` at `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/org-screens.jsx:192-252`. Build in the same structural style as this screen. Re-use the same page-layout for the list; render machine sequence chips inline using shadcn Badge variants in the same density tokens as the capacity bar pattern.

### Parity requirements (mandatory ACs)
The production implementation MUST match the prototype on three dimensions — add these as explicit ACs:
1. **Structural parity** — same sections, same field labels, same shadcn primitives invoked, same modals triggered from same actions.
2. **Visual parity** — same density tokens, same shadcn/Radix primitives (no drift to plain HTML), same layout proportions.
3. **Interaction parity** — same enable/disable rules, same loading/empty/error states, same keyboard focus order.

### Translation notes (from prototype-labels index)
- Hardcoded warehouse rows → Drizzle query on warehouses table joined with zones and bin_locations for counts
- Inline capacity bar (width % style) → shadcn Progress component; color threshold logic (>80% amber, >95% red) in server-computed field
- Storage rules section → useForm with zodResolver; Server Action for warehouse_settings upsert
- Toggle primitives (mixed lots, block expired) → shadcn Switch
- Expiry threshold number input → shadcn Input type='number' with min/max constraints from config
- Hardcoded labels → next-intl keys

### shadcn primitives to use
`Table`, `Progress`, `Switch`, `Input`, `Select`, `Button`, `Badge`

pipeline_inputs.description: Line List screen (SET-018): production_lines list with machine sequence preview chips and V-SET-62 activate guard.
pipeline_inputs.details:
PRD §12.2 SET-018: Line List with `machine sequence preview` capability. Machine chips ordered by line_machines.seq; bulk activate must hit T-029 activateLine which raises NO_MACHINE (V-SET-62) when no machines assigned — UI surfaces inline row-level error and proceeds with successful rows.

Additional acceptance detail preserved from compressed AC list:
- Given the caller lacks `settings.infra.update`, when they view the list, then bulk action buttons are disabled with an aria-label explaining insufficient permissions.
- UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.
out_of_scope: SET-019 Line Edit drag-drop machine assignment — separate screen
acceptance_criteria: Given the lines list renders, when a line has 4 assigned machines (seq 1..4), then the row shows 4 ordered chips in the preview column.
Given a line has 8 machines, when the row renders, then the first 6 chips show plus a `+2 more` indicator.

Given a line with 0 machines is bulk-selected and the user clicks Bulk Activate, when T-029 activateLine returns NO_MACHINE (V-SET-62), then the row shows an inline error and the action does not flip status.

Given the task is closed out, when reviewers inspect the task evidence, then these additional requirements are proven together: Given the caller lacks `settings.infra.update`, when they view the list, then bulk action buttons are disabled with an aria-label explaining insufficient permissions.; UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.
test_strategy: RED: RTL test mounts list with seed (line0=0 machines, line4=4 machines, line8=8 machines); asserts chip render + +N overflow + V-SET-62 inline error.
GREEN: implement Server Component + chips + bulk activate error rendering.
For UI parity, capture screenshots/artifacts and Playwright trace/video/artifacts where applicable; attach artifact paths in closeout.
risk_red_lines: Do not bypass V-SET-62 client-side; rely on T-029 server enforcement.
Do not implement Line Edit drag-drop (separate SET-019 screen).
